title: Saddle Peak National Park
tagline: Saddle Peak National Park — wildlife reserve of Andaman & Nicobar

heroImage.alt: Saddle Peak National Park, Andaman & Nicobar

overview.short: Saddle Peak National Park is a national park in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in India.
overview.description: Saddle Peak National Park is a national park in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in India. It was set up in 1979 on the surrounding areas of Saddle Peak. Saddle Peak or Saddle Hills is located on North Andaman Island.

topPlaces.name: Saddle Peak (Andaman Islands)
topPlaces.description: Saddle Peak or Saddle Hill is located on North Andaman Island in India's Andaman and Nicobar Islands. At 732 m, it is the highest point of the archipelago in the Bay of Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Kaplong Hydroelectric Project
topPlaces.description: Kaplong Hydroelectric Project (Kalpong Dam) is the largest dam of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands. Built across the Kalpong River at Nabagram Diglipur in the North and Middle Andaman district the dam was completed in 2001 and commissioned on 1 September 2001 by Shri M. Kannappan the Minister of State for Non-Conventional Energy Sources.

topPlaces.name: Ramnagar, Diglipur
topPlaces.description: Ramnagar (or Ram Nagar) is a village in the southeastern part of North Andaman Island. It belongs to the Diglipur county, Andaman and Nicobar territory of India.

topPlaces.name: Shibpur, Diglipur
topPlaces.description: Shibpur is a village located on North Andaman Island of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands in India. Administratively, it is a part of Diglipur tehsil of North and Middle Andaman district.

topPlaces.name: Khudirampur
topPlaces.description: Khudirampur is a village in Diglipur, North Andaman Islands.

items.note: Saddle Peak or Saddle Hill is located on North Andaman Island in India's Andaman and Nicobar Islands. At 732 m, it is the highest point of the archipelago in the Bay of Bengal.

Kaplong Hydroelectric Project (Kalpong Dam) is the largest dam of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands. Built across the Kalpong River at Nabagram Diglipur in the North and Middle Andaman district the dam was completed in 2001 and commissioned on 1 September 2001 by Shri M. Kannappan the Minister of State for Non-Conventional Energy Sources.
Ramnagar (or Ram Nagar) is a village in the southeastern part of North Andaman Island. It belongs to the Diglipur county, Andaman and Nicobar territory of India.
Shibpur is a village located on North Andaman Island of the Andaman & Nicobar Islands in India. Administratively, it is a part of Diglipur tehsil of North and Middle Andaman district.
Khudirampur is a village in Diglipur, North Andaman Islands.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Saddle Peak National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Saddle Peak National Park is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Saddle Peak National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Veer Savarkar International Airport, Port Blair (171 km) and the nearest railway station is Puri Railway Station (1064 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Saddle Peak National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Saddle Peak National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Saddle Peak National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Saddle Peak National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Saddle Peak National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Saddle Peak (Andaman Islands), Kaplong Hydroelectric Project, Ramnagar, Diglipur, Shibpur, Diglipur, Khudirampur.
